titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Facsimile of 1698 Kings County census ARC.264
titlestmt.author: Nicholas Pavlik
titlestmt.sponsor: This collection was processed and described as part of the project, "Uncovering the Secrets of Brooklyn's 19th Century Past: Creation to Consolidation," funded by the Council on Library and Information Resources, with additional support from The Gladys Krieble Delmas Foundation.

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2026-05-07 16:05:46 +0000.
profiledesc.langusage: Description is written in: English, Latin script.

did.abstract: Facsimile created in 1926 of a broadside containing the first census of Kings County, N.Y., recorded in 1698.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Identification of item, date (if known); Facsimile of 1698 Kings County census, ARC.264, Box and Folder number; Brooklyn Public Library, Center for Brooklyn History.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Broadside was flattened and sleeved in Mylar by John Zarrillo in March 2017.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Facsimile created in 1926 of a broadside containing the first census of Kings County, N.Y., recorded in 1698. The census lists the residents of each of Kings County's six original towns, including Brooklyn, Bushwick, Flatbush, Flatlands, Gravesend, and New Utrecht. Residents of the towns of Newtown and Flushing in Queens County are also listed. Coats of arms of prominent Kings County families are shown along the border of the broadside. A note on the verso indicates that the facsimile was created by Franklin Bittner.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: Material in this collection is in the public domain.

phystech.head: Physical Characteristics and Technical Requirements
phystech.p: If digital surrogates exist, they should be used in place of the originals whenever possible.

relatedmaterial.head: Related Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Center for Brooklyn History holds the following related materials:
- New York State 1892 Census returns for Brooklyn's 15th election district (1973.231), 1892
